english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: An ignoratis, fratres (scientibus enim legem loquor), quia lex in homine dominatur quanto tempore vivit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ ἀγνοεῖτε ἀδελφοί γινώσκουσιν γὰρ νόμον λαλῶ ὅτι ὁ νόμος κυριεύει τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐφ᾽ ὅσον χρόνον ζῇ
verses.king_james: Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam quæ sub viro est mulier, vivente viro, alligata est legi: si autem mortuus fuerit vir ejus, soluta est a lege viri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ ὕπανδρος γυνὴ τῷ ζῶντι ἀνδρὶ δέδεται νόμῳ ἐὰν δὲ ἀποθάνῃ ὁ ἀνήρ κατήργηται ἀπὸ τοῦ νόμου τοῦ ἀνδρός
verses.king_james: For the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to *her* husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of *her* husband.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur, vivente viro, vocabitur adultera si fuerit cum alio viro: si autem mortuus fuerit vir ejus, liberata est a lege viri, ut non sit adultera si fuerit cum alio viro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν ζῶντος τοῦ ἀνδρὸς μοιχαλὶς χρηματίσει ἐὰν γένηται ἀνδρὶ ἑτέρῳ ἐὰν δὲ ἀποθάνῃ ὁ ἀνήρ ἐλευθέρα ἐστὶν ἀπὸ τοῦ νόμου τοῦ μὴ εἶναι αὐτὴν μοιχαλίδα γενομένην ἀνδρὶ ἑτέρῳ
verses.king_james: So then if, while *her* husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque fratres mei, et vos mortificati estis legi per corpus Christi: ut sitis alterius, qui ex mortuis resurrexit, ut fructificemus Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀδελφοί μου καὶ ὑμεῖς ἐθανατώθητε τῷ νόμῳ διὰ τοῦ σώματος τοῦ χριστοῦ εἰς τὸ γενέσθαι ὑμᾶς ἑτέρῳ τῷ ἐκ νεκρῶν ἐγερθέντι ἵνα καρποφορήσωμεν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become dead to the law by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, *even* to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum enim essemus in carne, passiones peccatorum, quæ per legem erant, operabantur in membris nostris, ut fructificarent morti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε γὰρ ἦμεν ἐν τῇ σαρκί τὰ παθήματα τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν τὰ διὰ τοῦ νόμου ἐνηργεῖτο ἐν τοῖς μέλεσιν ἡμῶν εἰς τὸ καρποφορῆσαι τῷ θανάτῳ
verses.king_james: For when we were in the flesh, the motions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem soluti sumus a lege mortis, in qua detinebamur, ita ut serviamus in novitate spiritus, et non in vetustate litteræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ κατηργήθημεν ἀπὸ τοῦ νόμου ἀποθανόντες ἐν ᾧ κατειχόμεθα ὥστε δουλεύειν ἡμᾶς ἐν καινότητι πνεύματος καὶ οὐ παλαιότητι γράμματος
verses.king_james: But now we are delivered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not *in* the oldness of the letter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo dicemus? lex peccatum est? Absit. Sed peccatum non cognovi, nisi per legem: nam concupiscentiam nesciebam, nisi lex diceret: Non concupisces.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν ὁ νόμος ἁμαρτία μὴ γένοιτο ἀλλὰ τὴν ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ ἔγνων εἰ μὴ διὰ νόμου τήν τε γὰρ ἐπιθυμίαν οὐκ ᾔδειν εἰ μὴ ὁ νόμος ἔλεγεν οὐκ ἐπιθυμήσεις
verses.king_james: What shall we say then? *Is* the law sin? God forbid. Nay, I had not known sin, but by the law: for I had not known lust, except the law had said, Thou shalt not covet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Occasione autem accepta, peccatum per mandatum operatum est in me omnem concupiscentiam. Sine lege enim peccatum mortuum erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφορμὴν δὲ λαβοῦσα ἡ ἁμαρτία διὰ τῆς ἐντολῆς κατειργάσατο ἐν ἐμοὶ πᾶσαν ἐπιθυμίαν χωρὶς γὰρ νόμου ἁμαρτία νεκρά
verses.king_james: But sin, taking occasion by the commandment, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence. For without the law sin *was* dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem vivebam sine lege aliquando: sed cum venisset mandatum, peccatum revixit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ ἔζων χωρὶς νόμου ποτέ ἐλθούσης δὲ τῆς ἐντολῆς ἡ ἁμαρτία ἀνέζησεν ἐγὼ δὲ ἀπέθανον
verses.king_james: For I was alive without the law once: but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem mortuus sum: et inventum est mihi mandatum, quod erat ad vitam, hoc esse ad mortem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὑρέθη μοι ἡ ἐντολὴ ἡ εἰς ζωὴν αὕτη εἰς θάνατον
verses.king_james: And the commandment, which *was ordained* to life, I found *to be* unto death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam peccatum occasione accepta per mandatum, seduxit me, et per illud occidit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ ἁμαρτία ἀφορμὴν λαβοῦσα διὰ τῆς ἐντολῆς ἐξηπάτησέν με καὶ δι᾽ αὐτῆς ἀπέκτεινεν
verses.king_james: For sin, taking occasion by the commandment, deceived me, and by it slew *me*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque lex quidem sancta, et mandatum sanctum, et justum, et bonum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ὁ μὲν νόμος ἅγιος καὶ ἡ ἐντολὴ ἁγία καὶ δικαία καὶ ἀγαθή
verses.king_james: Wherefore the law *is* holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod ergo bonum est, mihi factum est mors? Absit. Sed peccatum, ut appareat peccatum, per bonum operatum est mihi mortem: ut fiat supra modum peccans peccatum per mandatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ οὖν ἀγαθὸν ἐμοὶ γέγονεν θάνατος μὴ γένοιτο ἀλλὰ ἡ ἁμαρτία ἵνα φανῇ ἁμαρτία διὰ τοῦ ἀγαθοῦ μοι κατεργαζομένη θάνατον ἵνα γένηται καθ᾽ ὑπερβολὴν ἁμαρτωλὸς ἡ ἁμαρτία διὰ τῆς ἐντολῆς
verses.king_james: Was then that which is good made death unto me? God forbid. But sin, that it might appear sin, working death in me by that which is good; that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scimus enim quia lex spiritualis est: ego autem carnalis sum, venundatus sub peccato.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν γὰρ ὅτι ὁ νόμος πνευματικός ἐστιν ἐγὼ δὲ σάρκικός εἰμι πεπραμένος ὑπὸ τὴν ἁμαρτίαν
verses.king_james: For we know that the law is spiritual: but I am carnal, sold under sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod enim operor, non intelligo: non enim quod volo bonum, hoc ago: sed quod odi malum, illud facio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ γὰρ κατεργάζομαι οὐ γινώσκω οὐ γὰρ ὃ θέλω τοῦτο πράσσω ἀλλ᾽ ὃ μισῶ τοῦτο ποιῶ
verses.king_james: For that which I do I allow not: for what I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem quod nolo, illud facio: consentio legi, quoniam bona est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ὃ οὐ θέλω τοῦτο ποιῶ σύμφημι τῷ νόμῳ ὅτι καλός
verses.king_james: If then I do that which I would not, I consent unto the law that *it is* good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem jam non ego operor illud, sed quod habitat in me peccatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ οὐκέτι ἐγὼ κατεργάζομαι αὐτὸ ἀλλ᾽ ἡ οἰκοῦσα ἐν ἐμοὶ ἁμαρτία
verses.king_james: Now then it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio enim quia non habitat in me, hoc est in carne mea, bonum. Nam velle, adjacet mihi: perficere autem bonum, non invenio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἶδα γὰρ ὅτι οὐκ οἰκεῖ ἐν ἐμοί τουτέστιν ἐν τῇ σαρκί μου ἀγαθόν τὸ γὰρ θέλειν παράκειταί μοι τὸ δὲ κατεργάζεσθαι τὸ καλὸν οὐχ εὑρίσκω
verses.king_james: For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh,) dwelleth no good thing: for to will is present with me; but *how* to perform that which is good I find not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim quod volo bonum, hoc facio: sed quod nolo malum, hoc ago.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ὃ θέλω ποιῶ ἀγαθόν ἀλλ᾽ ὃ οὐ θέλω κακὸν τοῦτο πράσσω
verses.king_james: For the good that I would I do not: but the evil which I would not, that I do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem quod nolo, illud facio: jam non ego operor illud, sed quod habitat in me, peccatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ὃ οὐ θέλω ἐγὼ τοῦτο ποιῶ οὐκέτι ἐγὼ κατεργάζομαι αὐτὸ ἀλλ᾽ ἡ οἰκοῦσα ἐν ἐμοὶ ἁμαρτία
verses.king_james: Now if I do that I would not, it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenio igitur legem, volenti mihi facere bonum, quoniam mihi malum adjacet:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὑρίσκω ἄρα τὸν νόμον τῷ θέλοντι ἐμοὶ ποιεῖν τὸ καλὸν ὅτι ἐμοὶ τὸ κακὸν παράκειται
verses.king_james: I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is present with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: condelector enim legi Dei secundum interiorem hominem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνήδομαι γὰρ τῷ νόμῳ τοῦ θεοῦ κατὰ τὸν ἔσω ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: For I delight in the law of God after the inward man:

verses.latin_vulgate: video autem aliam legem in membris meis, repugnantem legi mentis meæ, et captivantem me in lege peccati, quæ est in membris meis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπω δὲ ἕτερον νόμον ἐν τοῖς μέλεσίν μου ἀντιστρατευόμενον τῷ νόμῳ τοῦ νοός μου καὶ αἰχμαλωτίζοντά με τῷ νόμῳ τῆς ἁμαρτίας τῷ ὄντι ἐν τοῖς μέλεσίν μου
verses.king_james: But I see another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin which is in my members.

verses.latin_vulgate: Infelix ego homo, quis me liberabit de corpore mortis hujus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταλαίπωρος ἐγὼ ἄνθρωπος τίς με ῥύσεται ἐκ τοῦ σώματος τοῦ θανάτου τούτου
verses.king_james: O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.latin_vulgate: gratia Dei per Jesum Christum Dominum nostrum. Igitur ego ipse mente servio legi Dei: carne autem, legi peccati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐυχάριστῶ τῷ θεῷ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἄρα οὖν αὐτὸς ἐγὼ τῷ μὲν νοῒ δουλεύω νόμῳ θεοῦ τῇ δὲ σαρκὶ νόμῳ ἁμαρτίας
verses.king_james: I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.
